progress_summary: One-pass auditor sweep over the files touched by PRs #143, #146, #148, #151 (mobile workbench, mobile architecture diagram, welcome popup, full-bleed UI, top-right feedback pill, skill TOC restyle + click-to-flash, PostHog reverse-proxy). 16 findings reported, overall grade B+. The two CRITICAL severities flagged by the subagent (F-001 XSS via innerHTML on demo.astro, F-002 CSRF on /api/feedback) appear to be false positives on inspection — the innerHTML source is server-rendered Astro markup, and the feedback endpoint is same-origin with custom JSON content type. Real, actionable findings: F-005 (no prefers-reduced-motion guard on WelcomePopup), F-006 (close-button contrast in light mode), F-011 (Desktop/Mobile workbench duplication), and a dark-mode pressed-pill contrast issue surfaced by LHCI on /skills (Pill.astro line 50 has light-mode override only).

progress_table.label: Quality sweep — duplication, dead code, types
UX/a11y sweep — contrast, motion, focus, taps

key_decisions: Auditor mode only — no Fixer/Verifier this round (user did not request fix-all).
Subagent-delegated sweep due to ~8k LOC scope; auditor verified the two CRITICAL claims by reading the cited lines and downgraded both as likely false positives.

LHCI surfaced a real dark-mode contrast issue on .pill[aria-pressed=true] in Pill.astro — the existing B-10 override only fixes light mode (line 50). Dark-mode pressed-pill text is ~4.0:1 on the alpha-blended accent-dim surface, just under AA 4.5:1 for small text.
Default page theme is dark (Base.astro line 97), which is what LHCI audits.
user_preferences: Wants concrete findings with file:line, not generalities.
Comfortable triaging findings — doesn't need every CRITICAL fixed immediately if it's a low-likelihood false positive.

next_actions: User to choose scope for the dark-mode pill contrast fix: skip / add to PR #155 / open a separate one-line PR on Pill.astro mirroring the line 50 override into dark mode.
If a Fixer pass is wanted later: F-005 (add @media (prefers-reduced-motion: reduce) guard to WelcomePopup transitions) and F-006 (light-mode close-button color) are the cleanest wins.
F-011 (Desktop/Mobile workbench transcript duplication) is real tech debt — schedule for a refactor sprint, not a bug-fix branch.

headline_findings.title: No prefers-reduced-motion guard on WelcomePopup transitions
Dark-mode pressed pill ~4.0:1 contrast on /skills filter

skill_state.depends_on: checkpoint-protocol v1.0; reads ux-engineer + app-architect checkpoints for context
